text: Masters athletes (aged 40-81) who train 4-5 times per week do not show significant declines in mid-thigh muscle area or quadriceps area with increasing age.
Quadriceps peak torque (strength) does not decline significantly with age in chronically active masters athletes training 4-5 times per week.
Total body fat percentage increases with age even in highly active masters athletes, despite preservation of lean muscle mass.
Larger muscle cross-sectional area is significantly associated with greater quadriceps peak torque in masters athletes, confirming that maintaining muscle mass preserves functional strength.
Age-related muscle mass and strength loss commonly attributed to aging may primarily reflect chronic disuse (disuse atrophy) rather than an inevitable consequence of biological aging.
Specific strength (strength per unit quadriceps area) does not decline significantly with age in chronically exercising masters athletes.
Intramuscular adipose tissue does not significantly correlate with quadriceps strength in masters athletes who maintain chronic exercise habits.
